Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The European AllSky7 fireball network 107 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Embracing Bayesian Methods in Clinical Trials 19 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 981 upvotes, 198 comments
  4. Meow.camera 72 upvotes, 14 comments
  5. Sand from Different Beaches in the World 79 upvotes, 18 comments
  6. Installing a Let's Encrypt TLS Certificate on a Brother Printer with Certbot 136 upvotes, 39 comments
  7. We broke 92% of SHA-256 – you should start to migrate from it 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. Building FireStriker: Making Civic Tech Free 23 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. How and why to take a logarithm of an image [video] 159 upvotes, 57 comments
  10. Desk for people who work at home with a cat 197 upvotes, 78 comments
  11. PyPI package telnyx has been compromised in yet another supply chain attack 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  12. Can It Resolve Doom? Game Engine in 2k DNS Records 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  13. Iran-linked hackers have breached FBI director's personal emails 134 upvotes, 55 comments
  14. Gzip decompression in 250 lines of Rust 78 upvotes, 30 comments
  15. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 228 upvotes, 120 comments
  16. A Faster Alternative to Jq 330 upvotes, 202 comments
  17. AI got the blame for the Iran school bombing. The truth is more worrying 159 upvotes, 102 comments
  18. EMachines never obsolete PCs: More than a meme 45 upvotes, 23 comments
  19. Apple says no one using Lockdown Mode has been hacked with spyware 74 upvotes, 46 comments
  20. Hold on to Your Hardware 476 upvotes, 389 comments
  21. The 'paperwork flood': How I drowned a bureaucrat before dinner 459 upvotes, 379 comments
  22. Everything old is new again: memory optimization 136 upvotes, 103 comments
  23. Schedule tasks on the web 254 upvotes, 214 comments
  24. Apple discontinues the Mac Pro 601 upvotes, 562 comments
  25. People inside Microsoft are fighting to drop mandatory Microsoft Account 266 upvotes, 238 comments
  26. Last gasps of the rent seeking class? 99 upvotes, 91 comments
  27. ‘Energy independence feels practical’: Europeans building mini solar farms 107 upvotes, 103 comments
  28. Byte Magazine Archive 1975 to 1995 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Should QA exist? 47 upvotes, 80 comments
  30. 21,864 Yugoslavian .yu domains 33 upvotes, 57 comments

Last generated 3:00am, 28 March 2026. Source here.
